<!-- Thanks for contributing to CaptureImage! Please fill in every section. -->

## Summary

<!-- One or two sentences about what this PR does and why. -->

## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [ ] Documentation only
- [ ] Build / CI / release infrastructure
- [ ] Refactoring (no functional change)

## Related issues

<!-- Reference with `Fixes #123` / `Closes #456` / `Refs #789`. -->

## Testing

<!--
Describe how you verified the change. Minimum:
- `dotnet build CaptureImage.sln` and `dotnet test CaptureImage.sln` locally
- Manual repro of the bug (for fixes) or the new workflow (for features)

For UI changes, attach screenshots or a short GIF.
-->

- [ ] `dotnet build CaptureImage.sln` passes with 0 warnings / 0 errors
- [ ] `dotnet test CaptureImage.sln` passes all existing tests
- [ ] New tests added for new logic (if applicable)
- [ ] Manual smoke test performed (describe below)

## AI assistance disclosure

<!--
Check if the diff was materially generated with AI help. This does NOT
disqualify the PR — it just helps reviewers focus their attention. See
`docs/CONTRIBUTORS.md` for the project's AI assistance policy.
-->

- [ ] This change was produced with significant AI assistance
- [ ] This change is human-authored (no meaningful AI involvement)

## Checklist

- [ ] I have read [`docs/CONTRIBUTORS.md`](../docs/CONTRIBUTORS.md) and agree to license this contribution under GPL-3.0-or-later.
- [ ] I have updated `CHANGELOG.md` under the appropriate section.
- [ ] I have updated localization resources if the UI changed (`Strings.resx` + `.vi.resx` + `.ar.resx`).
- [ ] My commits follow conventional-commits style (`feat(...)`, `fix(...)`, `docs(...)`, etc.).
- [ ] I have not committed secrets, credentials, or personal screenshots.
